title: Blend early 2000s athletic aesthetic into the retro magazine design

requirements.summary: Introduce early 2000s athletic/sports visual accents (bold condensed fonts, jersey numerals, diagonal stripe motifs, sporty color accents) into the existing 1970s retro theme throughout the app while preserving the core newsprint aesthetic
acceptanceCriteria: At least one bold condensed sans-serif athletic font is applied to issue numbers, page indicators, or section headers throughout the app
Issue number on the Home page and page indicator in the Reader use a large jersey-style numeral treatment
At least two decorative CSS diagonal stripe or swoosh dividers appear in the layout (e.g., between masthead and nav, or between content sections)
Sporty color accents (crisp white, deep navy or black) are introduced as highlights without overriding the core 1970s earth-tone palette
Navigation hover effects incorporate at least one athletic-style treatment (underline swoosh, bold transform, or stripe flash)
The overall look reads as a 1970s retro magazine with sports-mag energy — not a modern sports site

file_operations.description: Add bold condensed sans-serif athletic font family (e.g., 'Impact' or condensed system stack) to the theme.fontFamily configuration. Add sporty color accents (crisp white as 'sportWhite', deep navy as 'sportNavy') to the theme.colors palette while preserving all existing 1970s earth tones (rust, mustard, avocado, paper, ink).

Import the athletic font family from Google Fonts or system fonts. Define CSS custom properties for sporty accent colors (--sport-white, --sport-navy). Add reusable Tailwind component classes for athletic styling: .athletic-heading (bold condensed font with tight letter-spacing), .jersey-number (extra-large bold condensed numeric style), .diagonal-stripe (CSS gradient or border treatment creating a swoosh/stripe divider), and .nav-athletic-hover (athletic-style hover effect with underline swoosh or bold transform). Ensure all new styles layer on top of existing retro base without overriding core newsprint textures or earth-tone palette.

Add a decorative diagonal stripe or swoosh divider below the masthead using the .diagonal-stripe class or inline Tailwind CSS with sporty accent colors. The divider should use CSS gradients or borders to create a dynamic angled visual element that contrasts with the retro striped borders but does not replace them.

Apply athletic-style hover effects to navigation links using the .nav-athletic-hover class or inline Tailwind utilities. Add a bold transform, underline swoosh, or stripe flash on hover that incorporates sporty accent colors (sportWhite, sportNavy). Ensure the base retro nav styling remains intact and the athletic treatment appears only on hover/active states.

Apply jersey-style numeral treatment to the issue number display using the .jersey-number class and athletic font family. The issue number should render as a large, bold, condensed numeric element with sporty color accents. Add a second decorative diagonal stripe or swoosh divider between the issue header and the cover feature section using CSS with the existing retro color palette plus sporty accents.

Apply jersey-style numeral treatment to the page indicator (current page number) using the .jersey-number class and athletic font family. The page number should be large, bold, and condensed with sporty color highlights. Ensure prev/next navigation buttons retain their retro base styling while optionally incorporating subtle athletic accents on hover.

Add at least one additional decorative diagonal stripe or swoosh divider between major layout sections (e.g., between navigation and main content area, or before the footer) using CSS gradients or borders with the retro palette augmented by sporty accents. Ensure the overall layout preserves the 1970s newsprint aesthetic and the athletic elements feel like decorative overlays rather than a rebrand.
